github_api_guide.goal: Track when the linked GitHub user pushes commits to selected repositories, especially inside a chosen organization

supported_approach.summary: Use GitHub OAuth to authenticate the user, let them choose repositories, then install a repository webhook on each selected repository and subscribe to push events
supported_approach.why: This gives a user-approved access token and real-time notifications for repository activity

authentication.note: OAuth app tokens act on behalf of the signed-in user
authentication.important_rule: Scopes only limit access. They do not grant access beyond what the user already has.

capabilities.capability: Authenticate a user with GitHub OAuth
Read the authenticated user's organizations and repositories they can access
Let the user choose repositories to monitor
Create one webhook per selected repository
Receive push events in real time
Filter push events by the linked GitHub username
Count commits and store metadata for challenge validation

flow.step: Send the user through GitHub OAuth
Store the returned access token and linked GitHub username
List repositories for the target organization
Let the user choose which repositories to monitor
Create a webhook on each selected repository
Receive webhook POST requests on your backend
Filter events where payload.sender.login equals the linked GitHub username
Increment counters or save commit metadata for challenge verification

get_authenticated_user.purpose: Resolve the linked GitHub username for later webhook filtering

repository_discovery.goal: List repositories the signed-in user can monitor inside an organization

endpoint.name: list organization repositories
endpoint.returns: Repositories in the organization visible to the authenticated user

endpoint.name: list user organizations
endpoint.returns: Organizations the authenticated user belongs to

repository_discovery.selection_rule: Only create webhooks on repositories the user selected and for which their token has enough access

webhooks.strategy: Create a repository webhook on each selected repository
webhooks.why: Repository webhooks give real-time push notifications and are better than polling for monitored repositories

create_webhook_endpoint.purpose: Create a webhook on a repository

webhook_handler_requirements.requirement: Accept POST requests at your configured payload URL
Verify the signature using the webhook secret
Parse sender, repository, ref, commits, and timestamps

webhook_filtering.secondary_rule_optional: Optionally also inspect payload.commits[*].author.username for stricter attribution
webhook_filtering.repo_match_rule: payload.repository.full_name must be one of the repositories selected by the user

use_cases.case: Fetch authenticated user profile after OAuth
List organizations for the signed-in user
List repositories in a selected organization
Optionally list existing hooks before creating a new one

endpoint.name: list user organizations
endpoint.purpose: Show organizations available to the user

endpoint.name: list organization repositories
endpoint.purpose: Show repositories that can be selected for monitoring

endpoint.name: list repository webhooks
endpoint.purpose: Check if a webhook already exists before creating another one

endpoint.name: create repository webhook
endpoint.purpose: Install the webhook on the selected repository

polling.needed: No for selected repositories with installed webhooks
polling.note: If you rely on repository webhooks, polling is not the primary strategy
polling.fallback: Polling can still be used for recovery, audits, or missed-delivery backfill

javascript_instructions.goal_for_llm: Generate JavaScript code that performs GitHub OAuth, stores the user token and username, lists org repositories, creates repository webhooks, receives push events, verifies signatures, filters pushes by linked username, and returns normalized commit events

required_capabilities.item: Exchange the OAuth code for a user access token
Call GET /user to resolve the GitHub login
Call GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/hooks to detect existing hooks
Call POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/hooks to install a webhook
Verify X-Hub-Signature-256 using the configured webhook secret
Return normalized commit records for storage or challenge checking
